title: XLSX Workbook Generation
description:
When inquiries are categorized, I want a formatted XLSX workbook output, so that I can quickly review, filter, and hand-edit categorizations in a familiar spreadsheet interface

Acceptance Criteria:
- Output is a valid .xlsx file named by the user or defaulting to inquiries_categorized.xlsx
- Inquiries sheet contains columns in order: Inquiry ID, Source, Received At (YYYY-MM-DD), Received At (Timestamp), From Name, From Email, Subject, Message Body, Predicted Category, Final Category, Notes
- Rows are sorted by Received At (Timestamp) in a consistent order (newest-first or oldest-first)
- Final Category column has data validation drop-down containing all configured categories
- Source column has data validation drop-down containing all source options: Intercom, In-app contact form, Discord, Direct email inquiry, LinkedIn message
- Final Category defaults to the Predicted Category value for each row
- Notes column is blank and editable

Topics of Concern:
- XLSX Workbook Generation: Generating a properly formatted Excel workbook with the Inquiries sheet, data validations, conditional formatting, and styling

Notes: A helper Lists sheet may be used to store category values and color mappings for the data validation references.

title: Category Configuration
description:
When I need to adjust inquiry categories or their visual styling, I want to edit a simple config file, so that categories can be changed without modifying code

Acceptance Criteria:
- Config file defines a list of category names
- Config file maps each category to a hex color value
- Default config includes all specified categories: Issue with login, Inquiry about plan or pricing, Request for data deletion, Wrong email address (not intended for Polymer), Advertising or solicitation, Knowledge base help, Feature help, Migration, Refund request, Other, Unclear
- Program loads categories and colors from the config file at runtime
- Program raises a clear error if the config file is missing or malformed

Topics of Concern:
- Category Configuration: A config file (JSON or YAML) that defines the list of categories and their associated color mappings

Notes: JSON or YAML format is acceptable. The config file should ship with sensible default colors for all default categories.

title: Inquiry Classification
description:
When I have a batch of normalized inquiries, I want each one automatically categorized, so that I can quickly review and approve predictions rather than categorize from scratch

Acceptance Criteria:
- Each inquiry receives a Predicted Category from the configured category list
- The classifier assigns 'Unclear' when it cannot confidently categorize an inquiry
- Classification uses the subject and message_body fields of the normalized record
- All predicted categories are valid entries from the config file's category list
- Classification runs without requiring external API calls or network access (or the approach is clearly documented if an LLM API is used)

Topics of Concern:
- Inquiry Classification: Auto-categorizing each normalized inquiry into one of the configured categories, including assigning Unclear when confidence is low

Notes: The spec input does not prescribe a specific classification approach (rule-based, keyword matching, or LLM-based). A confidence score is explicitly not required. The implementation should document which approach is chosen.

title: Record Normalization
description:
When inquiries arrive in different schemas, I want them normalized into a single internal record format, so that downstream processing (classification, XLSX output) operates on a consistent structure

Acceptance Criteria:
- Intercom inquiries map to normalized record: inquiry_id (string), source='Intercom', from_name from contact_name, from_email from contact_email, subject, message_body from first_message
- In-app contact form inquiries map to normalized record: inquiry_id (string of numeric id), source='In-app contact form', from_email from reply_to_email, subject, message_body from body
- received_at_date is formatted as YYYY-MM-DD for both input types
- received_at_ts for Intercom is derived from the Unix timestamp provided
- received_at_ts for in-app contact form is the YYYY-MM-DD date converted to a Unix timestamp at midnight (00:00:00) in a consistent timezone
- All normalized records have the same set of fields regardless of input source

Topics of Concern:
- Record Normalization: Mapping fields from each supported input format into a unified internal record with consistent field names and date formats

Notes: The from_name field may be empty for in-app contact form inquiries since that format does not include a name field. The timezone for midnight conversion should be documented or configurable.

title: Unit Tests
description:
When I make changes to the program, I want automated tests to verify core functionality, so that regressions are caught before they reach production

Acceptance Criteria:
- Tests verify parsing Intercom JSON into a correct normalized record
- Tests verify parsing in-app contact form JSON into a correct normalized record
- Tests verify received_at_date is YYYY-MM-DD for both Intercom (from Unix timestamp) and in-app contact form (from date string) inputs
- Tests verify received_at_ts is a correct numeric timestamp for both input types
- Tests verify the generated XLSX contains all required columns in the correct order
- Tests verify Final Category and Source columns have data validation (drop-down) applied
- Tests verify category color fills are applied to Predicted Category and Final Category cells
- Tests verify the Inquiries sheet rows are sorted by received_at_ts

Topics of Concern:
- Unit Tests: Pytest-based unit tests covering parsing, normalization, date handling, and XLSX output verification

Notes: Tests should use pytest. XLSX verification can use openpyxl to read the generated file and inspect structure, validations, and formatting.

title: Input Ingestion and Parsing
description:
When I have inbound inquiry data in various file formats and JSON shapes, I want the program to accept and parse them uniformly, so that I can process inquiries from different sources without manual reformatting

Acceptance Criteria:
- Program accepts one or more input files specified as command-line arguments
- Program accepts a JSON array piped via stdin or passed as a string argument
- Program correctly parses Intercom JSON shape with fields: id (string), state, subject, first_message (HTML), contact_email, contact_name, created_at (Unix timestamp)
- Program correctly parses in-app contact form JSON shape with fields: id (number), subject, body (plain text), reply_to_email, created_at (YYYY-MM-DD string)
- Program raises a clear error when input cannot be parsed or is missing required fields
- Program handles both single-object and array-of-objects JSON inputs

Topics of Concern:
- Input Ingestion and Parsing: Accepting input as JSON files, CSV files, or a JSON array via stdin/argument, and parsing the two supported JSON shapes (Intercom and in-app contact form)

Notes: CSV support is required but JSON will be the primary input format. HTML in Intercom first_message should be handled (at minimum preserved as-is in message_body).

title: Pre-commit Hook Setup
description:
When I commit code changes, I want unit tests to run automatically, so that broken code is never committed to the repository

Acceptance Criteria:
- A .pre-commit-config.yaml file exists in the repository root
- The pre-commit config includes a local hook that runs pytest
- The hook runs the full unit test suite on each commit attempt
- A failing test prevents the commit from proceeding
- The pre-commit framework (Python pre-commit package) is used to manage the hook

Topics of Concern:
- Pre-commit Hook Setup: Configuring the pre-commit framework to run pytest as a local hook before each commit

Notes: The pre-commit package (pre-commit.com) must be used rather than a raw .git/hooks/pre-commit script.

title: XLSX Formatting and Styling
description:
When I open the categorized workbook, I want it to be visually clear and easy to scan, so that I can efficiently review large batches of inquiries

Acceptance Criteria:
- Header row is frozen (stays visible when scrolling)
- Auto-filter is enabled on the header row
- Header row has bold text with a readable background fill
- Message Body column has text wrapping enabled and a wider column width than other columns
- All columns have reasonable default widths
- Predicted Category and Final Category cells are color-filled based on the category-to-color mapping from config
- Rows or category cells where Predicted Category or Final Category is 'Unclear' are visually highlighted distinctly

Topics of Concern:
- XLSX Formatting and Styling: Visual formatting of the workbook including header styling, column widths, text wrapping, color coding, and highlighting

Notes: Color fills should be applied per-cell based on the category value, not as conditional formatting rules (since the values are set at generation time for Predicted Category). Final Category color may need conditional formatting or be set to match Predicted Category initially.
